channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Egbetokun Still For Police IG House, Plots To Run For Ogun Senate After Forced Retirement
item.description: Despite being forced to retire as Inspector General of Police (IGP) by President Bola Tinubu, Kayode Egbetokun has continued to occupy the Police IG residence. Egbetokun, who hails from Erinja in Ogun...

item.title: Analyzing Trump's Strategy in Iran: Mixed Messages and Regional Impact
item.description: The article examines the mixed and often contradictory messages coming from the Trump administration regarding its actions in Iran. It questions the reasoning behind America's intervention in the regi...

item.title: Treasurer Orders ACCC to Scrutinise Petrol Price Gouging Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Amid escalating conflict in the Middle East, Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers has requested that the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) monitor petrol prices, warning retailers aga...

item.title: Rising Middle East tensions push oil prices up, affecting South African fuel costs
item.description: Oil prices have climbed sharply as tensions in the Middle East intensify, putting pressure on the rand and raising the prospect of higher fuel costs for South Africans. The conflict has escalated as t...

item.title: Canadian Solar Companies Power Rural Africa with Innovative Solutions
item.description: Canadian companies are leveraging solar technology and creative solutions to bring electricity to rural areas of Africa, where millions still live without access to reliable power. Firms like Toronto'...

item.title: Sonia Gandhi criticises PM Modi for silence after Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei killed
item.description: Congress Parliamentary Party chairperson Sonia Gandhi has sharply criticised the Indian government's silence following the assassination of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. In an opinion...

item.title: Contestant quits Channel 4's Handcuffed show after confrontation with producer
item.description: On 2 March 2026, the debut of Channel 4’s new reality show, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, ended in an unexpected confrontation. The show, hosted by Jonathan Ross, pairs strangers handcuffed together...

item.title: Global Markets Slide as Escalating Middle East Conflict Drives Oil Surge and Inflation Concerns
item.description: Global financial markets came under renewed pressure Tuesday as escalating conflict in the Middle East triggered a sharp rise in energy prices and heightened fears about inflation and economic stabili...

item.title: Surprising Biodiversity Found in the Atacama Desert's Soil Ecosystems
item.description: A recent study led by the University of Cologne has revealed that the Atacama Desert, one of the driest places on Earth, is home to diverse and resilient life beneath its surface. Researchers focused ...

item.title: Blood Moon Total Lunar Eclipse Visible Tonight Across Australia, New Zealand and North America
item.description: Tonight, a rare total lunar eclipse, known as a 'blood moon', will be visible across parts of Australia, New Zealand, and North America. The phenomenon occurs when the Earth aligns between the sun and...

item.title: Court Halts Ondo APC Congress Over Disputes, Bars INEC From Recognizing Past Congresses
item.description: The Federal High Court in Akure has issued a ruling to stop the All Progressives Congress (APC) from holding its planned state congress on March 3, 2026. The order, delivered by Justice Toyin Adegoke,...

item.title: Gender pay gap worsens at major Australian media companies, including SBS and News Corp
item.description: The latest Workplace Gender Equality Agency (WGEA) data reveal that some of Australia's largest media employers have seen setbacks in closing their gender pay gaps. This marks the third year of report...

item.title: Iran threatens to disrupt oil flow through Strait of Hormuz, potentially driving prices to $200 a barrel
item.description: Tensions between Iran and global oil markets have escalated following a warning from a senior Iranian general, Brigadier General Jabbari of the Revolutionary Guards, that Tehran will 'burn every ship'...

item.title: Court Dismisses Appeal Against Sanwo-Olu for Failure to Prosecute
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Lagos has dismissed an appeal filed by Prince Sammy Adebiyi and another party against Lagos State Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu and six other respondents. The court decision was b...

item.title: At least 10 killed as protesters storm US consulate in Karachi, Marines reportedly open fire
item.description: At least 10 people were killed on Sunday when protesters breached the outer wall of the US consulate in Karachi following the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in US-Isra...

item.title: AIRA LABS Aims to Revolutionize Local Governance with AI Technology
item.description: AIRA LABS, a Filipino AI company co-founded by Karl del Val and Jason dela Rosa, is pioneering the use of artificial intelligence in local government operations, with the goal of improving efficiency ...

item.title: Iranian Eyewitnesses Share Their Stories Amid War and Uncertainty
item.description: The sudden death of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has left the nation in a state of both jubilation and fear. While some celebrate, believing the death signals a chance for change, ot...

item.title: Government Student Loan Presentations Criticised for Misleading Comparisons
item.description: A BBC investigation has revealed that government-led presentations to teenagers in schools between 2011 and 2017 compared student loan repayments to £30-a-month phone contracts, an approach that is no...

item.title: The Ongoing Debate Over Scottish Independence in 2026
item.description: Scottish independence remains a deeply divisive issue, as demonstrated by a recent poll revealing that 47% of Scots would vote for independence, while 44% would vote against it. The referendum in 2014...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi calls for India’s moral clarity on Iran crisis, urges PM Modi to take a stand
item.description: In the midst of rising tensions between the United States, Israel, and Iran, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has urged the Prime Minister Narendra Modi-led government to take a clear and moral position. ...
